purpose: REVISION-2 companion assumption block for GLM-DROP (B1b), responding to the standing GPT-5.6 xhigh review of docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md (verdict FIX-FIRST; 9 blocking + 4 should-fix/minor findings, ALL resolved, none declined): (1) frozen analysis population, deterministic multilabel-to-analysis-cluster rule, cluster-balanced estimand, EXACT 2^8=256 sign-flip enumeration with F1-K's sign-symmetry basis and bootstrap fallback imported; (2) frozen per-layer universal-core algorithm with a <64 cap and the m-freq item-unconditioned correction to ASM-2290; (3) every open arm parameter pinned and the TOPP branch removed; (4) failed-superiority wording repaired -- no equivalence claim licensed without a separately powered test; (5) pilot moved to the F1-K dev-96 panel with a continuous statistic and aligned strict inequalities; (6) the standing sealed-checkpoint/fresh-process/separate-accounting isolation clause implemented literally with a shared-test-set freeze firewall; (7) correctness/performance regimes separated, loads/token the only systems endpoint, arm-level tok/s declined, P0 speed ratios re-scoped preliminary; (8) DROP redefined as an immutable item-ID-to-mask table with fail-closed lookup; (9) cost re-derived with R=3 preregistered derangements, instance-hours and dollars stated separately, ASM-2238 superseded in full; (10) per-mask-only footprint reporting; (11) closed 4-tag schema with the ASM-2291/2292 splits; (12) supersession map so no contradicted central clause remains operative; (13) full-SHA-256 evidence citations and stale central-register prose corrected. EMITTED by the Fable architecture-design agent (designer-12, 2026-07-13) for central registration by the coordinator in registry/assumptions.jsonl with the landing commit, after the standing GPT-5.6 review gate. The ASM-2230..2239 and ASM-2290..2293 companion files are left byte-intact as the pre-review records. This pass writes NO registry entry and takes NO git action; nothing is frozen, pre-registered, scheduled, or run; no model run and no spend occurs; no feasibility conclusion is stated anywhere in the block.

assumptions.claim: FROZEN ANALYSIS POPULATION + ESTIMAND + EXACT INFERENCE (fixes review finding 1; supersedes the decision-rule/inference content of ASM-2292, the stipulated-analysis content of ASM-2291, and ASM-2235's '10,000 resamples' statistics clause): (1) ELIGIBILITY, deterministic: an item is eligible iff its G-lex active-concept set is non-empty AND every active concept maps to a trace-covered cluster under a frozen, hashed kernel-concept-to-trace-cluster table; NO sampling step -- n = |eligible test items| exactly (<=1,440 by construction from the frozen F1-K test-1440 id list); the eligible-item ID list, its sha256, the exact n, the per-analysis-cluster counts m_c, and the exclusion count are freeze-manifest entries committed at prereg-freeze. Coverage gates fail to the maintainer BEFORE main-phase spend: exclusions >20% of the test pool; n < 300; any analysis cluster with m_c < 10. (2) ANALYSIS CLUSTERS, deterministic multilabel rule: every eligible item gets exactly ONE analysis cluster = the covered cluster of its highest-G-lex-trigger-score active concept, ties to the concept earliest in the frozen table order; C = 8 non-overlapping clusters by construction, realized non-empty count and m_c published at freeze. (3) ESTIMAND: cluster-balanced theta = (1/8) x sum_c E[D_c] with D_c the cluster mean of per-item paired differences d_i in {-1,0,+1}; the +3-point floor applies to the statistic T = mean_c D_c; the item-weighted accuracy contrast is descriptive only and any divergence is disclosed. (4) EXACT INFERENCE: with C = 8 the cluster sign-flip reference set is enumerated in full -- all 2^8 = 256 sign vectors, one-sided p = #{s: T(s) >= T_obs}/256 including identity; attainable p-values are k/256 and the registered reject-iff-p<=0.05 rule has effective size 12/256 ~ 0.0469 (conservative, disclosed); licensing per named contrast is the joint rule p <= 0.05 AND T >= +3. No Monte-Carlo resampling of an enumerable set. (5) VALIDITY BASIS IMPORTED from F1-K ASM-2122 / glm52-followup-experiment.md R-REV4.1a verbatim: type-I exact under cluster sign-symmetry for any within-cluster ICC; pre-registered FALLBACK = cluster (concept-block) BCa bootstrap of T with 95% CI and the same +3 floor; the enumeration-vs-bootstrap choice is frozen at prereg-freeze on the DEV sign-symmetry check, never on test data. [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 1): (i) the concept->trace-cluster table is MATERIALIZED and hashed in the design (section 2.1): 8 rows, canonical serialization '<idx>:<concept>:<fingerprint ids>' LF, sha256:10cb109d651727d89bbb575ae39690e1cb4a0081dd373a9eec139986e7ac5e38; a G-lex concept outside the table makes its items INELIGIBLE. (ii) 'highest-G-lex-trigger-score' was not executable (G-lex emits spans, not scalars); REPLACED by the executable rule importing F1-K's registered span precedence verbatim: A(i) = the cluster of the active concept owning the span with the longest matched trigger surface (Unicode code points), tie -> earliest span start, tie -> earliest row in the frozen table. (iii) The eligible-ID list, n, m_c and exclusion count are an explicitly SEQUENCED-AFTER-F1-K-FREEZE pin, not an open hole: they are a pure function of F1-K's frozen test-1440 id list + trigger map + span sidecars, which are #28/F1-K freeze outputs verified this tick to not yet exist as repo bytes; the function, gates, and fail-closed default (no eligible-ID hash => freeze cannot complete => no spend) are frozen here, and section 6.1 step 5 orders GLM-DROP's freeze before any F1-K test outcome.]
assumptions.rationale: The review showed the R1 record left n, the eligible set, the cluster construction, the estimand, and the reference distribution all open -- every one of them an outcome-relevant degree of freedom; freezing them as deterministic rules whose realized values are hash-committed at prereg-freeze, and enumerating the 256-element reference set exactly instead of sampling it, removes the freedom and states the test's granularity honestly.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md sections 5.1, 5.2 [R2] + section R2 item 1; GPT-5.6 review finding 1; docs/next/design/glm52-followup-experiment.md R-REV4.1a / ASM-2122 (sign-symmetry basis + BCa fallback, imported); ASM-2342 (C = 8 measured basis)

assumptions.claim: POWER PLANNING PROJECTIONS (the projection half of the ASM-2292 split; never premises): under the joint rule at C = 8, with SE^2 = (sigma_d^2/n)(1+(m-1)rho), m = n/8, SE floors at sigma_d x sqrt(rho/8); at the F1-K planning premise family (sigma_d ~ 39 pts at 15% paired disagreement, rho = 0.10): n = 300 gives SE ~ 4.8 pts and MDE_true ~ 12 pts; n -> infinity gives SE ~ 4.3 pts and MDE_true ~ 10.8 pts; honest joint power at a true +3 effect ~ 0.15 at n = 300 and <= 0.5 at any n (the observed-margin ceiling). MDE_true = max(3, c_alpha x SE) + 0.842 x SE with c_alpha x SE the Gaussian planning boundary 1.645 x SE -- the EXACT boundary of the 256-flip enumeration is data-dependent and is NOT computable from these planning inputs. The withdrawn 0.90-power-at-true-+3 target appears nowhere. These figures exist so the maintainer can see the resolution scale before dollars move (MD-6); the named power lever is C (more trace clusters), not n. [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 1): the PINNED PLANNING SIMULATION now EXISTS and RAN at the planning point (design Appendix A): code sha256:1dc1682a0cf37cec00b1f0f4444e54c27d6944b2fe70a870b7730b1ec801e3d4, seed 20260728, 20,000 replicates/grid point, ALL 2^8=256 sign flips enumerated per replicate, canonical output sha256:9cdd2c01d300b648d0ad7c30cdb3320b53731755046b98725e98e82f5ede1769, double-run byte-identical. Pinned planning results: joint power at true +3 = 0.119; simulated MDE_true at 0.80 joint power = 16.0 pts at n=300 (0.5-pt grid) -- HARSHER than the Gaussian ~11-12-pt figures above, which the simulated values SUPERSEDE as the design's planning resolution (the Gaussian rows remain as derivation trail only; every resolution-scope sentence in design section 5.3 quotes ~16 pts). This tightening is reported in the non-demonstration direction, never softened.]
assumptions.rationale: The R1 record mis-tagged these projections as a load-bearing stipulation; they are planning arithmetic over assumed sigma/rho and an approximate rejection boundary, so they must be registered as a non-load-bearing projection with an explicit mechanical resolution path, per the review's finding 11 split.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md section 5.2 [R2] + section R2 items 1/11; GPT-5.6 review findings 1 and 11; docs/next/design/glm52-followup-experiment.md R-REV4.1(b)/R-REV5.1 (joint-MDE precedent and its Gaussian-approximation caveat); ASM-2340 (the frozen inference these projections plan for)
assumptions.resolution_path: [R3-updated] PARTIALLY RESOLVED this pass: the pinned simulation ran at the planning point (code/output hashes in the claim; Gaussian figures superseded by simulated MDE_true ~16 pts). FULLY resolved by the SEQUENCED-AFTER-F1-K-FREEZE RE-RUN of the SAME pinned code with only the m_c line replaced by the frozen realized values (that one-line diff plus new code+output sha256s are freeze-manifest entries); the re-run's simulated MDE_true and power-at-true-+3 become the MEASURED power record the maintainer decides on (MD-6). Never resolved against quality outcomes or the cost ledger.

assumptions.claim: MEASURED EVIDENCE BASE for the R2 design decisions (the measured half of the ASM-2291 split; full hashes, exact keys/lines): (a) probe coverage = 24 prompts spanning 8 concept clusters, 3 prompts/cluster (poc/glm52-probe/concept_prompts.json sha256:64df95a0095c892386e9ba71d3cc5be41d957e43cf308348d81eae248e81047f; fingerprint roster in poc/glm52-probe/results/probe-main.log sha256:77023d2b94e5f7719bdf86955de26cfb720ad33eb53521b48d47e50bc2a52592, fingerprint list ending 'fingerprints written: 24' at line 51) -- the unit the map can condition on is the CLUSTER, so C = 8; (b) universal component = 10,591 of 16,483 ever-active (layer, expert) cells carrying 93.92% of mass (poc/glm52-probe/results/routing-analysis-v2.json sha256:e9d6813f99a6efa03645a3c15260a84c3d47221a3d0061ad6bbc68749e1c57cc, keys /universal_cells = 10591, /keyspace = 16483, /universal_mass_frac = 0.9392) -- an average of ~139 universal cells per MoE layer across 76 layers [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13: the R2 text said '~141 across 75 layers'; the committed stats files span MoE layers 3-78 = 76 layers (re-derived this tick from the roster-pinned bytes; roster sha256:e90a8d6e32bcd3abe8d4a7ef2334695d82ff5c0dd9670b83a8e853a5115cfcc0); also for citation precision: 0.9392 is the MEAN of the 24 per-fingerprint universal-mass fractions (the pooled-mass fraction is 0.9303, computed this tick)], so retaining ALL universal cells inside a 64-expert/layer budget is impossible and the core must be a strict subset; (c) concept-shaped routing structure: mean-centred within 0.9119 vs across -0.0740, perm p = 0.0001 at 10,000 shuffles seed 20260712 (same file, keys /mean_centered/within, /mean_centered/across, /mean_centered/perm_p_10k, /nperm, /seed); (d) P4 TOPK sweep counters (probe-main.log section '#### P4 TOPK shrink-lever sweep', lines 52-71): experts loaded/token 1825.1 / 1596.0 / 998.9 / 606.3 and expert hit rate 33.0% / 38.8% / 41.8% / 44.9% at TOPK 8/6/4/2, tok/s 0.10 / 0.12 / 0.19 / 0.25 -- ONE sequential run with monotonically rising hit rate, so the 1.9x/2.5x tok/s ratios are preliminary observations, and the P1 baseline counter 1786.5 (lines 12/18, TOPK=0 default) is a DIFFERENT measurement from P4's TOPK=8 value 1825.1 (line 57), a conflation present in R0/R1 prose and corrected in R2.
assumptions.rationale: Splitting the measured facts from the design choices they motivate (per review finding 11) keeps the MEASURED tag honest -- every number here is committed bytes with a full hash and an exact key or line -- and pins the two facts the R2 algorithms depend on: the core cannot be 'all universal cells', and the P0 speed ratios cannot be read as causal arm-level estimates.
assumptions.backing_ref: poc/glm52-probe/results/routing-analysis-v2.json sha256:e9d6813f99a6efa03645a3c15260a84c3d47221a3d0061ad6bbc68749e1c57cc (primary evidence file, hash re-verified against committed bytes 2026-07-13; keys enumerated in claim); poc/glm52-probe/results/probe-main.log (lines enumerated in claim); poc/glm52-probe/concept_prompts.json; docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md sections 0, 2.1, 2.2, 4.1, 4.3 [R2]

assumptions.claim: FROZEN UNIVERSAL-CORE ALGORITHM + m-freq SCOPE CORRECTION (fixes review finding 2; supersedes ASM-2233's sized-at-pilot core clause and ASM-2290's four-arm matched-multilabel clause): (1) CORE, per MoE layer l: rank all 256 experts by pooled trace mass (sum over the 24 fingerprints) descending, ties by ascending expert index; core(l) = the shortest prefix with cumulative mass >= 50% of layer l's pooled mass, HARD-CAPPED at 48 experts (75% of the 64-expert/layer budget), guaranteeing >= 16 slots/layer for the concept-conditional tail. tau = 0.50 and cap 48 are a-priori design constants fixed in this record, not tuned; the IDENTICAL core bytes are embedded in every masked arm. The core is necessarily a strict subset of the 10,591 universal cells (~139/layer average across 76 layers, ASM-2342 as R3-amended); the R1 wording implying full universal retention is superseded. [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 2): the core is MATERIALIZED, no longer deferred to freeze -- the frozen rule was EXECUTED this pass on the roster-pinned fingerprints (roster sha256:e90a8d6e32bcd3abe8d4a7ef2334695d82ff5c0dd9670b83a8e853a5115cfcc0): 76 layers, 2,108 total core cells, per-layer sizes min 15 / max 46 / mean 27.7, NO layer reaches the 48 cap (every layer keeps >= 18 concept-tail slots, so the >=16 guarantee holds with margin); canonical serialization = one ascii line per layer ascending, '<layer>:' + comma-joined ascending expert indices + LF; canonical core sha256:632bcd182ae8a716b3f8843d640d43772f9ddfac6880d12ac531cf974cd3f18f, published with the full per-layer size vector in design section 2.1. The freeze manifest copies this hash verbatim and the freeze-time regeneration must be byte-identical (mismatch = fail-closed halt).] (2) m-freq CORRECTION: the matched-multilabel clause of ASM-2290 applies ONLY to the item-conditioned arms m-kern / m-emb / m-drng; m-freq is dose-matched and core-matched but INTENTIONALLY ITEM-UNCONDITIONED -- it consumes no per-item labels, which is precisely what makes it the workload-blind importance deflator. Dose (exactly 64/layer) and the shared core remain invariant across all four masked arms.
assumptions.rationale: The review proved 'retain the universal core' could not literally mean all universally-active cells (141/layer average vs a 64 budget) and that ASM-2290 mis-described m-freq's structure; freezing a deterministic, capped, hash-pinned core rule that provably leaves tail room, and restating m-freq's intentional unconditionedness, closes both defects without touching the dose invariant that makes D2 attributable to labels.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md sections 2.1, 3 [R2] + section R2 item 2; GPT-5.6 review finding 2; ASM-2342 (universal-cell counts); ASM-2290 (the dose protocol this corrects in part)

assumptions.claim: PINNED MASK-CONSTRUCTION AND GATE PARAMETERS (fixes review finding 3; supersedes ASM-2232's Jaccard clause and TOPP reference, ASM-2234's load-gate ambiguity, and completes ASM-2290): (1) m-emb encoder = sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2, CPU, weights content-hash pinned in the freeze manifest; input text = the item's question stem verbatim (exactly the text G-lex scans, options excluded), Unicode NFC, whitespace collapsed, stripped, NO case-folding; embeddings L2-normalized; the same rule embeds the 24 trace prompts. (2) Trace clustering = k-means, k=8, kmeans++ init, seed 20260724, n_init=10 (lowest inertia; tie -> lowest init index), max_iter=300; assignments + centroids (member means, L2-renormalized) frozen and hashed. (3) Nearest-cluster rule = descending cosine(item embedding, centroid), top-|C(item)| clusters, ties -> lower cluster index. (4) Derangements: R = 3 preregistered realizations, seeds 20260721/20260722/20260723, deranging the item-to-active-concept-SET assignment within equal-cardinality strata; each realization is a seeded Fisher-Yates shuffle rejection-resampled (max 10,000 attempts, then fail-closed halt) until fixed-point-free AND set-distinct (sigma(i)'s label set differs from item i's AS A SET -- no identical wrong-label sets); items in strata where no valid assignment exists (singleton strata, or all items sharing one label set) are excluded from m-drng only and counted; exclusions > 10% of eligible n render D2 leg (c) UNINFORMATIVE; D2(c) is evaluated paired on the non-excluded subset with m-kern restricted to the same items, testing m-kern against the per-item MEAN over the 3 realizations. (5) Jaccard non-distinctness gate: J(i,l) over retained sets; GATE statistic = mean over all eligible items x all MoE layers (m-drng: pooled over realizations); mean > 0.95 -> pair non-distinct -> that D2 leg UNINFORMATIVE; per-layer means and per-item max reported descriptively, non-gating. (6) Load gate level: ARM-LEVEL MEANS -- mean realized experts-loaded/token over the arm's full scored item set within +/-5% of u-topk's mean on identical items; per-item ratios descriptive; violation VOIDs the arm. (7) TOPP branch REMOVED (the review's removal option): no TOPP value exists anywhere in the design; escalation is k=1 only, then STOP. All seeds are freeze-manifest entries disjoint from F1-K's seed sets. [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 3, residuals): (a) PRNG PINNED -- all run-time seeded randomness uses the GLM-DROP-DRBG (design section 3.1 item 8): draw t of ascii stream L = first 8 bytes big-endian of SHA-256('glm-drop:' + L + ':' + decimal(t)); uniform ints by rejection (accept iff u < 2^64 - (2^64 mod k)); uniforms as u*2^-64; seeds enter via stream labels (drng:<seed>:<cardinality>:<attempt>, kmeans:20260724:<init>, blocks:20260726:<arm>:<pass>, spotset:20260727); no library PRNG anywhere. (b) k-means IMPLEMENTATION pinned: the harness's OWN deterministic implementation to the frozen spec in section 3.1 item 2 (float64, squared-Euclidean on L2-normalized vectors, kmeans++ via the DRBG, tie/empty-cluster/convergence/inertia rules all stated) -- no library, no floating version. (c) Encoder weights: fail-closed SEQUENCED-AT-FREEZE pin -- the artifact is not repo bytes (verified this tick); at freeze the enumerated model files are fetched and each file sha256 recorded; missing/changed artifact HALTS the freeze; m-emb is never built from unpinned weights and D2 leg (b) is never silently waived. (d) The three derangement permutations are a pure function of (eligible set, seeds, DRBG) and are materialized + hashed at prereg-freeze -- SEQUENCED-AFTER-F1-K-FREEZE because the eligible set is (ASM-2340 as amended). (e) Previously unnamed seeds NAMED and the registry CLOSED: 20260726 block-order, 20260727 correctness spot set, 20260728 planning power sim (a pinned code artifact with its own internal counter-based generator); randomness outside the registry is a config error and halts at bring-up.] [R4 amendment, designer-20, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 3 residual): the MULTILABEL COMBINATION RULE is pinned -- ONE deterministic rule for all item-conditioned arms (design sections 2.1 item 3, 3, 3.1 item 3). Scoring functional: per-cluster mean-centred conditional mass s_c(l,e) = mean over the cluster's member fingerprints of x_f(l,e), minus mu(l,e); x_f = the fingerprint's histogram L1-normalized over the FULL 16,483-cell ever-active keyspace (the analyze_routing_v2.py construction behind the pinned /mean_centered/* numbers; absent and out-of-keyspace cells = exactly 0.0), mu = the elementwise mean over the in-scope roster (24 fingerprints; the LOO-reduced roster where section 2.1 LOO fires, with F_c and mu recomputed by the same formulas); float64 throughout, all sums in ascending fingerprint-id order. Combination: S_item(l,e) = the PLAIN UNWEIGHTED SUM of s_c(l,e) over the item's conditioning clusters in ascending cluster-index order -- no per-concept quota, no max, no |C(item)| normalization (cardinality is matched across the item-conditioned arms, so a constant factor changes no ranking); |C(item)|=1 reduces to the single-concept ranking verbatim. Selection: per layer, rank all non-core experts by S_item descending, exact float64 comparison, ties -> ascending expert index (the frozen tie-break); retained set = core(l) union the first 64-|core(l)| experts of that ranking -- exactly 64/layer, a total order always (never-active experts score exactly 0.0), and NO residual trim step exists (the core is fixed and the tail is one ranked prefix, so no union-of-top-r ambiguity can arise). m-emb: the SAME rule verbatim with the frozen k-means cluster member sets (item 2) in place of concept-cluster member sets, the conditioning set = the item's top-|C(item)| nearest clusters (item 3), summed in ascending k-means cluster-index order; cosine affinity only SELECTS clusters and carries NO weight in the score (affinity-weighted variants explicitly rejected as an added degree of freedom); no LOO arises (benchmark items are never trace prompts). m-drng: the m-kern rule verbatim applied to sigma(i)'s label set. The three item-conditioned pipelines now differ ONLY in cluster member sets and item->label assignment; no ambiguity that changes a realized mask remains.]
assumptions.rationale: Every parameter here was an open, outcome-relevant choice the review enumerated; pinning algorithm, version-by-hash, input text, seeds, tie-breaks, aggregation rules, and fail-closed fallbacks before any dev result is read is what makes the deflator contrasts preregistered rather than selectable, and deleting the unspecified TOPP branch is cheaper and cleaner than freezing a grid the design does not need.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md section 3.1 [R2] + section R2 item 3; GPT-5.6 review finding 3; ASM-2290 (dose protocol completed); ASM-2293 (the D2 legs these parameters serve)

assumptions.claim: OPERATING-POINT PILOT, STRENGTHENED + ALIGNED (fixes review finding 5; supersedes ASM-2234's operating-point clause): pilot panel = F1-K's frozen dev-96 split reused verbatim (id-list hash pinned in the freeze manifest), with the GLM-DROP-eligible subset identified per ASM-2340's rule; GATE: >= 48 eligible dev items, else return to the maintainer before further spend; statistic granularity (100/n_dev-eligible pts/item; ~1.04 pts at 96) disclosed next to every pilot number -- the 24-item / 4.17-pt-increment / one-item-rule pilot is superseded. Selection rule, deterministic and frozen before any test access: run b0-full and u-topk at k in {4,3,2} on the panel; k* = the LARGEST k whose dev accuracy deficit acc(b0-full) - acc(u-topk@k) on eligible items is STRICTLY > 3.0 points; escalation if none: k = 1 (one further cell; TOPP branch removed per ASM-2344); if no tested point exceeds 3.0, STOP and report 'no uniform-truncation deficit > 3 points was observed on the dev panel at k in {4,3,2,1}' -- scoped to panel and tested k values, and NEVER worded 'retention-free' (an unpowered equivalence claim, ASM-2346). INEQUALITIES ALIGNED: the pilot requires dev deficit > 3.0 and D0 floor-binds at test deficit <= 3.0 -- a deficit of exactly 3 is insufficient in BOTH places. UNCERTAINTY, reported non-gating: prespecified continuous statistic = mean paired difference in correct-option log-likelihood margin (LL(correct) - max wrong-option LL) between b0-full and u-topk@k, with a seeded (20260725) 10,000-draw nonparametric item bootstrap 90% CI; if its sign disagrees with the accuracy-rule selection at k*, the pilot surfaces to the maintainer BEFORE freeze (fail-closed).
assumptions.rationale: A 24-item panel made the k* selection a one-item rule and the R1 inequality pair let a dev-accepted operating point be test-floor-bound at the identical deficit; quadrupling the panel to the already-frozen F1-K dev split, disclosing granularity, adding a continuous statistic with uncertainty, and making both thresholds strictly-greater-than-3 removes the noise and the inconsistency while keeping dev-only selection.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md section 4.2 [R2] + section R2 item 5; GPT-5.6 review finding 5; docs/next/design/glm52-followup-experiment.md (frozen dev-96 split); ASM-2340 (eligibility rule); ASM-2346 (STOP wording discipline)

assumptions.claim: OUTCOME-WORDING + EQUIVALENCE DISCIPLINE (fixes review finding 4; supersedes ASM-2236's negative-outcome sentences): a failed one-sided superiority test is NEVER read as equivalence, sufficiency, deadness, or 'added nothing'; this design powers NO equivalence test, so NO outcome licenses 'X adds nothing', 'retention-free', 'the kernel is one conditioner among several', 'mask structure did the work', or 'dead'. Frozen replacements: (a) not-D1 -> 'a guided-vs-uniform advantage >= +3 points was not demonstrated at the registered resolution (simulated MDE_true ~16 pts at the n=300 planning point, C=8 -- pinned simulation, refreshed at freeze at realized n, m_c [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13: figure updated from the Gaussian ~11-12 pts to the pinned simulated value per ASM-2341 as amended; the equivalence discipline itself is untouched]) on this subset; uniform TOPK stands as a colibri-native, concept-free lever with no kernel sentence attached', and B1b is recorded NOT-DEMONSTRATED-at-this-resolution-scale-and-subset; (b) D1 with D2 not demonstrated -> 'kernel-guided expert-drop beat uniform truncation, but a kernel-SPECIFIC advantage over the kernel-free conditioned deflators was not demonstrated at the registered resolution', reported at equal prominence; (c) D2 leg (c) miss -> 'a kernel-label-specific advantage over dose-exact deranged labels was not demonstrated at the registered resolution'. D2 FAIL-TO-DEMONSTRATE (legs ran cleanly, joint rule missed) and D2 UNINFORMATIVE (a leg voided by the Jaccard, dose, load, or derangement-exclusion gates) are DISTINCT recorded outcomes; UNINFORMATIVE is neither a pass nor a deflation. Every non-demonstration sentence carries its resolution scope. If the programme later wants an equivalence/sufficiency claim on this seam it requires a separately powered TOST/non-inferiority design with its own margin and preregistration -- not this experiment's nulls.
assumptions.rationale: The R1 decision table did exactly what its own statistics section forbade -- reading nonsignificance at an 11-12-point MDE as 'added nothing'; freezing non-demonstration wording with the resolution scope attached, keeping FAIL distinct from UNINFORMATIVE, and requiring a separately powered equivalence design for any sufficiency sentence is the only wording set the design's actual power can license.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md sections 4.2, 5.3 [R2] + section R2 item 4; GPT-5.6 review finding 4; ASM-2341 (the resolution scale quoted in the sentences); ASM-2293 (D2 conjunction, untouched)

assumptions.claim: CO-LOCATION ISOLATION PROTOCOL, IMPLEMENTED LITERALLY (fixes review finding 6; supersedes ASM-2237's 'sequenced AFTER F1-K's arms complete' clause; implements docs/next/design/glm52-f1k-cost-reduction.md line 30 verbatim): mandatory ordered steps, each logged in GLM-DROP's own manifest: (1) F1-K's arms complete AND its durable checkpoint is SEALED (checkpoint sha256 recorded in F1-K's manifest) before any GLM-DROP engine process starts; (2) F1-K's engine process is TERMINATED, PID and exit logged -- no process survives the boundary; (3) GLM-DROP starts under a NEW PID (logged) with KAE, TOPK, TOPP, DROP, PIN, AUTOPIN explicitly RESET to GLM-DROP's frozen values and VERIFIED by an environment echo captured at startup into the manifest; inherited F1-K knob state is a halt condition; (4) distinct namespaces: glm-drop/checkpoints/, glm-drop/results/, glm-drop/manifest.json, and a separate cost ledger glm-drop/cost-ledger.json -- no file shared with F1-K's namespaces, accounting separable by construction; (5) FREEZE-ORDER FIREWALL: GLM-DROP's eligible items are a subset of F1-K's test-1440, so GLM-DROP's prereg freeze must be committed BEFORE F1-K's test-set outcome files are generated; if scheduling makes that impossible, F1-K's test outcomes are firewalled -- read by no GLM-DROP role, firewall and any access recorded in both manifests -- until GLM-DROP's freeze commit lands; (6) never interleaved inside an arm.
assumptions.rationale: The standing clause requires seal + fresh process + own manifest and accounting, and the shared test set makes freeze ordering load-bearing: an unsealed handoff or an early read of F1-K's test outcomes would let GLM-DROP's design absorb outcome information from the very items it will score; implementing each requirement as a logged, verifiable step makes the isolation auditable rather than aspirational.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md section 6.1 [R2] + section R2 item 6; GPT-5.6 review finding 6; docs/next/design/glm52-f1k-cost-reduction.md line 30 (the standing clause); ASM-2237 (superseded in part)

assumptions.claim: CORRECTNESS REGIME vs PERFORMANCE REGIME (fixes review finding 7; supersedes ASM-2234's 'fresh caches at arm boundaries' + tok/s spot-check clauses and ASM-2235's tok/s clause; scopes the P4 reading of ASM-2231): (1) CORRECTNESS: scoring cache-independence is VERIFIED, not asserted -- at bring-up a seeded 8-item spot set is scored cold (fresh process, caches dropped) and warm (after preload) and per-option logits must be BYTE-IDENTICAL; any mismatch halts for a protocol amendment; given the check passes, page-cache state during scored runs is unconstrained (cost lever only); AUTOPIN/.coli_usage stay off (no adaptive state); the PIN set = the universal core, IDENTICAL across all arms including b0-full and u-topk, never pilot-selected, recorded in the manifest. (2) PERFORMANCE: realized experts-loaded-per-token (the engine's deterministic, routing-determined, cache-independent counter) is the ONLY systems endpoint of this experiment; arm-level tok/s comparisons are EXPLICITLY DECLINED and the R1 <=3-prompt generative tok/s spot-check is REMOVED; the P0 1.9x/2.5x ratios are cited only as preliminary observations from one sequential 8->6->4->2 run with hit rate rising 33.0->38.8->41.8->44.9% (ASM-2342), never as causal arm-level estimates; any future arm-level throughput claim requires its own preregistered protocol (replicated fixed-length prompts, balanced arm order, identical RAM budgets, fixed reset/preload/warm-up rule, exclusion of warm-up/interrupted blocks, uncertainty intervals) -- not this design.
assumptions.rationale: The R1 text simultaneously demanded fresh caches (4.3) and warm caches (6.2) because it conflated the scoring regime with the cost regime; verifying byte-identical cold/warm logits makes cache state provably scoring-irrelevant, and declining tok/s comparisons in favour of the deterministic loads counter removes the endpoint the cache/pinning/order confounds could bias -- the cheaper and more honest of the review's two offered resolutions.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md sections 4.3, 6.2 [R2] + section R2 item 7; GPT-5.6 review finding 7; ASM-2342 (P4 counters and the sequential-run caveat)

assumptions.claim: ITEM-ID-TO-MASK TABLE (fixes review finding 8; supersedes ASM-2239's read-once single-mask design and its 40-60-line estimate): DROP=<table-file> is an IMMUTABLE item-ID-to-mask table loaded ONCE at engine start -- a sidecar mapping item-ID to per-layer 256-bit expert bitmasks (one entry per eligible item per masked-arm realization; uniform arms carry no table); the harness passes the item-ID with each scoring request and the engine resolves the mask by FAIL-CLOSED lookup: an unknown or missing ID refuses the request and halts the run, never a silent fall-through to unmasked routing. Integrity: per-entry sha256 over each (item-ID, bitmask) record in the table header, verified at load; whole-table sha256 recorded in both the run manifest and the freeze manifest; the table is bit-identical bytes for the entire run (no reload path). Per-item engine restarts are REJECTED as the realization mechanism (they would multiply bring-up overhead and invalidate the co-location cache economics); a future pass preferring restarts must preregister and price them explicitly. Revised estimate ~120-200 lines against the same pinned colibri commit (a78a06fc5acc4b0dc0f9ef03987c66b0559d1250); still one C change, in-repo diff, inert unless DROP is set, fail-closed on malformed tables, bundled into the #28 maintainer patch-approval review; NOT a Law-1 matter (selects among native experts, writes nothing into activations) but exceeds the P0 trace-dump-only envelope (ASM-1986/1989 carried). [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 8, residuals): (a) the per-entry and whole-table hashes are an explicitly SEQUENCED-AFTER-F1-K-FREEZE pin -- each arm-realization's table is a pure function of (eligible-ID list [ASM-2340], core + concept->cluster hashes [in-document], section 3.1 parameters/seeds/DRBG, pinned encoder bytes for m-emb), so the tables cannot exist before F1-K's freeze by construction; fail-closed default: the engine refuses to start unless the loaded table's whole-table sha256 equals the freeze-manifest value (no code path runs a masked arm on an unverified table). (b) The C-patch approval is a NAMED EXTERNAL GATE (the #28 bundled maintainer review), not a design hole; fail-closed default: while unapproved there is NO bring-up, NO freeze completion, NO spend (design section 7 item 4 prerequisite); a refusal returns the design to the maintainer with per-item restarts priced as the review's alternative.] [R4 amendment, designer-20, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 8 residual): the CANONICAL SERIALIZATION and HASH FRAMING are pinned NOW (design section 6.3 [R4] block) so each table is a PURE FUNCTION of its inputs in bytes, not just in content. (1) One file per (masked arm, realization), arm-ID in the closed set {m-freq, m-emb, m-kern, m-drng-1, m-drng-2, m-drng-3}; m-freq's entries are identical across items by construction but still emitted per item (one loader path, per-item dose verification). (2) Encoding: all-ASCII, LF endings, no BOM, decimal unpadded integers, lowercase hex. (3) Item-ID: the F1-K frozen id string VERBATIM as UTF-8 bytes; an id containing '|', ':', a control byte, or non-ASCII is a config error -> halt. (4) Per-layer expert-bit order: the 76 MoE layers ascending (3..78), all present; each layer's 256-bit mask = 64 hex chars c_0..c_63, c_j encoding experts 4j..4j+3 with expert 4j at the nibble MSB (value 8) and 4j+3 at the LSB (value 1), bit 1 = retained; exactly 64 retained bits/layer verified at load (dose invariant), violation halts. (5) Record line: '<item-id>|<layer>:<64-hex>|...' over the 76 layers ascending, LF-terminated; record order = ascending byte-lexicographic item-ID (unsigned bytewise). (6) Per-entry hash = SHA-256 over ascii 'glm-drop-mask:' + arm-ID + ':' + the record line exclusive of its trailing LF (arm-ID in-frame so entries cannot be transplanted between arm tables undetected). (7) File layout, byte-exact: line 1 'glm-drop-table/1:<arm-ID>:<record-count>:<section-2.1 core sha256>'; then per-entry hash lines '<item-id>|<sha256 hex>' in canonical order; then the separator line '--'; then the record lines in the same order; every line LF-terminated. (8) Whole-table hash = SHA-256 over the COMPLETE file bytes exactly as loaded; copied into the freeze and run manifests; the engine re-hashes at start and refuses on mismatch, then re-verifies every per-entry hash (both fail-closed). SEQUENCING made explicit: with ASM-2344 as R4-amended pinning every score/tie-break/seed/DRBG-draw and items (1)-(8) pinning every byte, table materialization and its hashes remain SEQUENCED-AFTER-F1-K-FREEZE solely because the eligible-item ID LIST is (ASM-2340 as amended); the serialization spec and hash framing wait on nothing, freeze-time regeneration must be byte-identical, and the freeze pass makes NO serialization choice.]
assumptions.rationale: The review proved the R0 startup-mask design could not realize per-item masks at all -- the design's central mechanism was unimplementable as written; an immutable, hash-verified, fail-closed lookup table is the smallest engine change that makes every masked arm executable in one process without touching the cost assumptions, and pricing honesty requires admitting it is more code than the R0 estimate.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md section 6.3 [R2] + section R2 item 8; GPT-5.6 review finding 8; ASM-2239 (superseded in part); ASM-2290 (the per-item dose verification this table serves)

assumptions.claim: COST RECORD, RE-DERIVED (fixes review findings 9 + 12; supersedes ASM-2238 IN FULL -- its degradation clause contradicted ASM-2293, its band was not derived from the standing model, it confused dollars with hours, it named no derangement count, and its resolution path pointed at quality metrics): derangement policy R = 3 preregistered realizations (ASM-2344; F1-K's registered R=3 precedent -- averages realization noise in D2(c) without pricing mapping-precision repetitions), so the main phase is 8 pass-equivalents (b0-full, u-topk, m-freq, m-emb, m-kern, 3 x m-drng). Standing F1-K model (100 s/prefill pessimistic / 1.20 pinning divisor / $0.20-0.28 per h spot): pilot <= ~530 prefills (dev-96 pilot <= 480 + ~50 bring-up cold/warm verification); main = 8 x n; n = 300 planning point -> ~2,930 prefills -> ~68 INSTANCE-HOURS -> ~$14-19 SPOT DOLLARS; n = 1,440 pool cap -> ~12,050 prefills -> ~279 instance-hours -> ~$56-78 spot dollars (consistent with the reviewer's independent arithmetic of $9-13 / $41-57 at six passes and ~$76 at the R=3 high corner). BAND ~ 68-279 instance-hours ~ $14-78 spot dollars; CEILING = 320 instance-hours AND $95 spot dollars, whichever binds first; on-demand fallback at $0.69/h ~ $193-220 worst case, a coordinator sign-off decision, never silent. Degradation order if the ceiling binds, consistent with ASM-2293: (1) drop the k=1 escalation pilot cell; (2) return to the maintainer -- NO masked arm dropped, R never below 3, n never cut below the frozen basis. Mask/map construction CPU ~$0; S3 retention on the existing $8.5/mo line. All figures planning-only, never premises. [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 12): the superseded central clauses are quoted VERBATIM so the coordinator's transcription is exact. Central ASM-2238 (registry/assumptions.jsonl line 1090, read this tick) clause (a), VOID: 'Pre-registered degradation order if the ceiling binds: (1) drop m-rand; (2) drop m-freq (m-emb, the stronger conditioned deflator, is never dropped); (3) drop the escalation k-cell; (4) return to the maintainer' -- contradicts the registered D2 conjunction (ASM-2293); replaced by this record's order. Clause (b), VOID and REPOINTED: 'Resolved by the measured GLM-5.2 expert-drop run ...; the projection is discharged by the runs quality-retention metrics under the frozen deflator policy' -- this record resolves ONLY against the metered cost ledger glm-drop/cost-ledger.json, never quality metrics. INTERIM PRECEDENCE RULE, binding on every GLM-DROP role until the coordinator's transcription lands: any conflict between central ASM-2238 and this record resolves to this record; central ASM-2238 is cited by no GLM-DROP artifact except to record this supersession. The central-register edit itself is the coordinator's landing-commit action (this pass writes no registry byte).]
assumptions.rationale: A cost record that is not derived from the standing model, mixes units, omits the derangement multiplier, and resolves against quality metrics cannot gate spend honestly; re-deriving from the registered F1-K model with hours and dollars separated, the R multiplier explicit, and resolution against the metered ledger makes the ceiling auditable and the degradation order consistent with the D2 conjunction.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md section 6.4 [R2] + section R2 items 9/12; GPT-5.6 review findings 9 and 12; docs/next/design/glm52-f1k-cost-reduction.md (standing model + R=3 precedent); ASM-2293 (no-arm-dropped rule); ASM-2344 (R = 3)
assumptions.resolution_path: Resolved by the metered spot-instance spend recorded in GLM-DROP's OWN cost ledger (glm-drop/cost-ledger.json, ASM-2347 namespace) at run completion, cross-checked against F1-K's bring-up-measured s/prefill; if measured throughput or spot rates push the projection over the ceiling, the registered degradation order governs and the ceiling is re-derived from measured throughput. Never resolved against quality-retention metrics.

assumptions.claim: FOOTPRINT REPORTING RULE (fixes review finding 10; supersedes the f x 370 GB ~ 93 GB shard clause of ASM-2235 and the '~93 GB retained pool' clause of ASM-2237): the only footprint reported per arm is the PER-MASK routed-expert footprint; the R1 'projected deployment shard size ~93 GB' and 'masked arms draw from a ~93 GB pool' claims are WITHDRAWN -- a per-item 25% mask implies neither a single deployable shard nor one fixed cache pool, because the UNION footprint across the frozen workload's per-item masks can be much larger. The union footprint across all per-item masks of the frozen workload is MEASURED at pilot and reported BEFORE any shard-size or page-cache-pool statement is made; no shard or cache-pool claim attaches to this experiment, and any cache-benefit expectation in the cost design is scoped as a hope resolved by the measured union footprint and hit rate.
assumptions.rationale: The 93-GB figure silently equated a per-item fraction with a workload-wide pool, which both overstates a deployment implication and props up the cache-economics story; reporting per-mask only and measuring the union before any claim keeps the systems observations inside what the design actually measures.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md sections 5.1, 6.2 [R2] + section R2 item 10; GPT-5.6 review finding 10; ASM-2348 (loads/token as the only systems endpoint)

assumptions.claim: GOVERNANCE CORRECTIONS: TAG SCHEMA + SPLITS + CITATION RULE + STALE PROSE (fixes review findings 11 + 13): (1) the design's tag convention and self-check are corrected to the CLOSED four-tag schema MEASURED | LIT-BACKED | STIPULATED | EXTRAPOLATION; DERIVED and ASSESSMENT are retired -- arithmetic is stated inline with its premises' tags, and designer judgment appears only as explicitly non-binding prose, never as a tag. (2) ASM-2291 is SPLIT: measured coverage fact -> ASM-2342 (MEASURED); stipulated analysis consequences -> ASM-2340 (STIPULATED). (3) ASM-2292 is SPLIT: frozen inference/decision record -> ASM-2340 (STIPULATED); planning projections -> ASM-2341 (EXTRAPOLATION, load_bearing:false, resolution_path to the pinned freeze simulation); the simulation result itself enters the record as MEASURED only at prereg-freeze with code+output hashes, never before it exists. (4) CITATION RULE: every MEASURED claim in the design cites an artifact with FULL sha256 (header table) plus the exact JSON key or log line/section; truncated hashes and bare paths are non-conforming. (5) STALE PROSE corrected: ASM-2230..2239 and ASM-2290..2293 ARE centrally registered (registry/assumptions.jsonl lines 1082-1091 and 1106-1109); the design's earlier 'central register not written' wording described R0/R1 emission state and is superseded. (6) The central_supersessions section of this file is the coordinator's transcription map; after transcription no superseded clause of ASM-2232/2233/2234/2235/2236/2237/2238/2239/2290/2291/2292/2293 remains simultaneously operative with its replacement. [R3 amendment, designer-17, 2026-07-13 (re-review finding 11, residuals): (a) the ASM-2291/2292 splits are COMPLETE as the review's taxonomy requires -- STIPULATED frozen rule (ASM-2340), non-load-bearing EXTRAPOLATION with resolution_path (ASM-2341), and the simulation result now MEASURED at the planning point (ASM-2341 as amended; Appendix A of the design) with the realized-m_c re-run still to-be-MEASURED at freeze; (b) the design's tag convention carries no DERIVED/ASSESSMENT token (grep-verified this tick; the tokens appear only in audit prose describing their retirement); (c) central registration of ASM-2340..2352 requires a registry write + landing commit, both outside this pass's brief -- it is explicitly classified SEQUENCED-WITH-LANDING-COMMIT, coordinator-owned, and the design is NOT freeze-eligible before it lands; (d) this block was amended in place ONLY because it is not yet centrally registered (in-place amendment of a registered block is prohibited; post-registration changes are supersede-by-citation), with pre-R3 bytes hashed in the r3_amendment field.]
assumptions.rationale: Mixed-tag records, an open tag vocabulary, truncated hashes, and stale registration prose each create a place where a later reader can honestly mis-derive what is measured versus chosen versus projected; closing the schema, splitting the mixed records, requiring full-hash+key citations, and correcting the register state keep the assumption ledger mechanically auditable.
assumptions.backing_ref: docs/next/design/glm52-expert-drop.md header tag convention + sections R2.11/R2.13/R2.14 + self-check gate [R2]; GPT-5.6 review findings 11 and 13; registry/assumptions.jsonl lines 1082-1091 and 1106-1109 (read this tick)

central_supersessions.note: Transcription map for the coordinator (this pass writes NO registry entry). Mark each listed clause superseded in registry/assumptions.jsonl at landing so no contradicted clause remains operative; line numbers as read this tick at repo HEAD 3611025a3580b13cfb69af1d31e3723b9b9d165c.

entries.superseded_clauses: m-rand arm definition + single-nearest-cluster m-emb rule (already superseded per R1 by ASM-2290); Jaccard gate without an aggregation rule; any TOPP escalation reference

'universal core sized at pilot (top cells by pooled mass until cumulative mass >= a pre-registered fraction, frozen in the prereg)' -- the core algorithm is now frozen at design time with tau=0.50/cap 48; '~24 concepts' coverage wording

operating-point selection clause (24-dev-item pilot, deficit >= 3 acceptance, TOPP-tightened-k=2 escalation); 'fresh caches at arm boundaries' and the tok/s spot-check; unstated load-gate aggregation level

power clause (already superseded per R1 by ASM-2292, itself now split into ASM-2340/2341); '10,000 resamples' statistics clause (replaced by exact 256-flip enumeration); item-unit estimand ambiguity; '<=3-prompt seeded generative tok/s spot-check' and 'projected shard size f x 370 GB' clauses

D2 max/stronger-deflator clause (already superseded per R1 by ASM-2293); the outcome sentences 'concept guidance added nothing', 'dead-at-this-scale-and-subset', 'the kernel is one conditioner among several', and the m-rand 'any-mask regime' flag

'sequenced AFTER F1-K's arms complete' isolation wording (replaced by the sealed-checkpoint/terminate/new-PID/env-verified/distinct-namespace/freeze-firewall protocol); 'masked arms draw from a ~93 GB retained pool'; the asserted (unverified) 'scoring is cache-state independent'

ENTIRE RECORD: degradation order permitting dropping m-rand/m-freq contradicts ASM-2293; band not derived from the standing model; '$60 spot-hours' unit confusion; no derangement count; resolution_path pointing at quality-retention metrics
read-once 'DROP=<mask-file>' single-bitmask design (cannot realize per-item masks) and the 40-60-line estimate
